entry.title: what are the alternative options for VBS scripts since it will be deprecated by Microsoft?
entry.content:
I'm contacting you to confirm the future direction of SAP Scripting (VBS-based), an automation feature for SAP.

As Microsoft has indicated its intention to gradually deprecate VBS,

I would like to confirm whether SAP Scripting can continue to be used, or whether it is scheduled for deprecation.

If it is going to be deprecated, I would also appreciate it if you could also let me know:

- Are there any alternative automation methods (officially recommended tools)?

- If so, what are their overviews?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: what are the alternative options for VBS scripts sinc...
entry.content:
Hello customer,

Indeed there is a VBScript deprecation timeline from Microsoft as outlined in the VBScript deprecation: Timelines and next steps | Windows IT Pro Blog . As you can see there, the "Feature removal from OS" is To Be Defined (TBD).

The implications of the future VBScript deprecation in the SAP Scripting is explained in the SAP Note  3484031 - "Deprecation of VBScript by Microsoft - impact on SAP GUI Scripting"."

"At the moment VBScript is still part of Microsoft Windows and there is no concrete timeline for removing the feature completely. Should VBScript be missing in future versions of Windows it will for a limited period of time still be possible to activate it (see also https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/whats-new/deprecated-features). Thus, it is our recommendation to make use of this activation as long as possible, because only if VBScript is available, you will be facing no impact on the usage of SAP GUI Scripting in SAP GUI for Windows. You can use the time to prepare by migrating any scripts recorded via SAP GUI Scripting using VBScript to another language "

Fortunately, in the upcoming SAP GUI for Windows 8.10 release, an alternative scripting language, JScript, is supported for script replaying, alongside VBScript, to ensure compatibility with SAP GUI for Java. 

As VBScript may become obsolete, SAP has enabled JScript recording in the latest release. The type of script recording, JScript or VBScript, is determined by the file extension used during saving: ".js" for JScript and ".vbs" for VBScript. Currently, there are no plans to support additional scripting languages like PowerShell, though this could change based on demand.

To prepare for a potential phase-out of VBScript, it’s advisable to manually migrate existing scripts to JScript. While automatic conversion isn’t supported, the transition is relatively straightforward due to the similar syntax between VBScript and JScript.

I hope I was able to clarify your questions and concerns.


 
Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Creating a DQM Validation Rule for Business Partner Role Assignment in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Overview

Data quality is critical in Master Data Governance (MDG), especially when dealing with Business Partners. One common data quality issue occurs when Business Partners are created without any Business Partner Role assigned. Since roles determine how a Business Partner participates in business processes (customer, supplier, etc.), missing roles can break downstream applications.

The goal is to create a DQM validation rule in SAP Master Data Governance that prevents the creation or modification of Business Partners without role assignments. The system will show a validation error or warning as shown in the following screenshot:

The validation logic works as follows:

  1. The rule iterates over Business Partner records in table BUT000.
  2. For each Business Partner ID, a table operation checks table BUT100.
  3. If no entry exists in BUT100 for that BP, the validation rule raises an error.

This ensures that every Business Partner has at least one role assigned before it can be saved.

Prerequisites

Before implementing the rule, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Master Data Governance with Data Quality Management (DQM) enabled
  • User authorization with the SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_STEWARD role or equivalent
  • Access to the SAP Fiori application Validation Rules for Business Partners (App IDs F4084 or F4082A (newer releases)

Rule Configuration

Step 1 — Access the DQM Validation App

  1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Launch the Validation Rules for Business Partners app.

This UI/App allows you to manage and create validation rules for Business Partner data

Step 2 — Create a New Validation Rule

Click Create and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Rule ID

CHECK_BP_ROLE_MANDATORY

Rule Name

BP Role Assignment Required

Base Table

BUT000 (Business Partner General Data)

Checked Field

BUT0BK-PARTNER_ROLE (optional but recommended)

The base table must be BUT000, even though the validation checks data stored in another table

Step 3 — Maintain General Information

Fill in the business metadata for the rule:

Description
Ensures every Business Partner has at least one role assigned.

Reason
Business Partners without roles cannot participate in business processes.

Rule Owner
Data Steward responsible for Business Partner data.

Implementation Expert
Technical contact responsible for BRF+ implementation.

Save the rule by clicking Create.

Step 4 — Technical Implementation

Configure Rule Usage

  1. In the Usage section, select Add.
  2. Choose Check in Central Governance.
  3. Click Prepare to generate the BRF+ application.
  4. Select Send for Implementation.

This enables the rule logic to be edited.

Scope Expression Configuration

The Scope Expression defines which Business Partners should be validated.

Steps:

  1. Open the Scope Expression link.
  2. The Simplified BRF+ Workbench appears.
  3. Define the scope.

Example scope:

Partner Category = 2 (Organization)

This means the validation rule will only apply to organizational Business Partners.

You can also configure the rule to apply to all Business Partners.

Condition Expression Implementation

The main validation logic is defined in the Condition Expression.

Steps:

  1. Open the Condition Expression.
  2. Remove the dummy condition.
  3. Create a new expression.

Choose:

Expression Type:
Table Operation

Example name:

BUT100_CHECK

Table Operation Configuration

Configure the operation as follows:

Parameter

Value

Operation

Has at least

Table

Procedure Call

Procedure

BUT0BK lookup ALL

Condition

Partner Role is not initial

This configuration checks if at least one role exists for the Business Partner.

 

Although the rule loops over BUT000, the lookup ultimately checks role entries in BUT100.

This cross-table validation is the key technical detail of this solution.

Activation and Deployment

Once testing is complete:

  1. Select Send for Testing to change the rule status.
  2. After validation, choose Approve.
  3. Go to the Usage tab.
  4. Click Enable for Check in Central Governance.

The rule is now active and will validate Business Partner data during governance processes.

Testing the Rule

Test the validation logic.

Test Scenarios

Scenario

Expected Result

BP without roles

Validation error

BP with one role

Validation passes

BP with multiple roles

Validation passes

Testing ensures that only valid Business Partner records can be saved.

 

Troubleshooting

Rule Not Triggering

Check the following:

  • All BRF+ expressions are active
  • Rule status is Approved
  • Usage is Enabled

Performance Issues

If validation becomes slow:

  • Restrict the Scope Expression
  • Limit evaluation to specific Business Partner categories
  • Avoid unnecessary table operations

 

Best Regards

Steffen

entry.title: Re: Change Color of Edited (Unpublished) Cells in SAC Pla...
entry.content:
Unfortunately that particular CSS class is not exposed via the Story CSS, however I did create a custom widget a while back that allowed the Developer to modify the DOM of the Table at runtime. I modified the custom widget to allow the story developer to change the Modified Cell background color at runtime. 
You can access the Custom Widget here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ceq-GYaXPgp_Me_4BK8CTJieu7BUV58j?usp=sharing 
The custom widget has 4 methods exposed:

  1. setModifiedColorForAllTables(color:string) - this will change the modified color for all tables. You should be able to pass in HEX values (e.g. "#ccf4fd"), RGB values (e.g. "rgb(255, 223, 28)") or Color literals (e.g. "lightblue") as well as RGBA and HSL values should you need them.
  2. setModifiedColorForSelectedTables(tables:Table[],color:string) - this is similar to the first method but allows you to set the changes only on specific Tables on the page. In this case you would pass in an array of Table objects in addition to the color string.
  3. clearModifiedColorForAllTables() - reverts the modified color back to the default for all tables
     
  4. clearModifiedColorForSelectedTables(tables:Table[]) - reverts the modified color back to the default for the selected tables
 
Once you have the custom widget added to your tenant you can simply add it to your story (Note: custom widgets are not Global objects and must be added to each page, however as this custom widget has no front-end it adds no additional load time).
Below are some screenshots of the user experience that you can expect.
This first screenshot was taken after adding the following lines of code to my page onInitialization() event and then changing a value in the Table:
ModifiedColor_1.setModifiedColorForAllTables("#ccfdf8");
 
 
This next image is after clicking the Change Color button which has the following code:
var newColor = InputField_1.getValue();
ModifiedColor_1.setModifiedColorForAllTables(newColor);

The changes happen without refresh because we are changing the DOM itself. Finally this last screenshot was taken after clicking the Revert Color button which had the following code:

ModifiedColor_1.clearModifiedColorForAllTables();

For information on deploying Custom Widgets to your tenant check out the SAP Analytics Cloud Help guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/0ac8c6754ff84605a4372468d002f2bf/1c71e80bf2b440d4a2267a473f46ed2f.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

Sean

entry.title: Re: Synchronized scrolling for two or more tables
entry.content:
As previously mentioned, this capability is not available out of the box. However, I have created a custom widget in the past that did capture the users scrolling behavior for another use case. I updated this custom widget to support synchronized scrolling. I haven't fully tested every scenario but the basics appear to be working. 

You can download the custom widget from here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1irb8HNx1pIXUcH9bzWpKpvHE_NLSOo-S?usp=sharing 

The widget itself contains two methods:

  1. bindTables(tables:Table[],scrollLink:string) - this method takes in an array of Table objects as well as a string representing the type of scroll link that you want to bind. Valid values for scroll link are "vertical", "horizontal" and "both". When a link is established any of the linked tables can be scrolled and all of the other tables will respond accordingly. 
  2. removeAllBindings() - removes all of the bindings from all of the tables
 

Some notes that I have noticed in my brief testing using the custom widget:

  • It works best when the tables are the same size, at least in the scroll direction. I am using % in the scroll to help handle this as much as possible but it is definitely not perfect if the tables are not of similar size
  • You will need one instance of the widget for each binding. For example, if I wanted to bind Table_2 and Table_3 horizontally and Table_5 and Table_6 vertically, then I would need to add two instances of the custom widget and add code similar to the following to the page's onInitialization event:
SyncTableScroll_1.bindTables([Table_2, Table_3], "horizontal");
SyncTableScroll_2.bindTables([Table_5, Table_6], "vertical");​

Below are some screenshots that I took when testing the scrolling:
Vertical Scrolling:

After Vertical Scroll

Horizontal Scrolling:

Before ScrollAfter Horizontal Scroll

If you need instructions on how to upload a custom widget to your tenant, you can reference this section in the Help guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/0ac8c6754ff84605a4372468d002f2bf/1c71e80bf2b440d4a2267a473f46ed2f.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

Hope this helps

entry.title: Pregúntame lo que quieras con SAP Champions – Serie mensual (Sesión 2)
entry.content:
Hola querida SAP Community,

Mi nombre es María Antonieta López, SAP Champion de México.

Actualmente trabajo como Directora de Programas SAP, enfocada en programas globales de transformación SAP, implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA, arquitectura empresarial, marcos de gobernanza e innovación digital dentro del ecosistema SAP.

Como parte del equipo de SAP Champions que lidera la iniciativa “Ask Me Anything” junto con Sebastian Marchesini,

🚀yo estaré liderando la edición de marzo.

Así que sí…

Puedes preguntarme. LO QUE SEA.

 

Después de más de 20 años viviendo dentro de transformaciones SAP, probablemente he visto lo bueno, lo malo… y esos momentos de “¿cómo sobrevivió este sistema al go-live?” 😅

 

Durante las próximas semanas estaré respondiendo cualquier pregunta que tengan — sin filtros — solo SAP en vivo y real.

💡Podemos explorar temas como:

⚙️Implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA y programas globales de transformación.

🏗️ Arquitectura Empresarial para SAP utilizando enfoques basados en TOGAF.  Artefactos, ¿cómo medir la madurez, SAP Lean IX, etc...

🧼 Clean Core en SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public) — procesos de gestión de proyectos para implementar o crear extensiones o interfaces en tu proyecto.

(Hablabdo de BUILD para configuradores y su uso con Joule, APIs preconstruidas, APIs nuevas, y por supuesto nuestra plataforma como servicio SAP Integration Suite, todo dentro de nuestra querida SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)).

☁️RISE o GROW con SAP + Gobernanza y metodología Clean Core

Desde SAP Activate (la reina 👑) hasta marcos como SAP Application Extension Methodology, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology), SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework y, por qué no… incluso SAP Business AI Framework.

Puedes preguntar sobre qué artefactos usar, cómo ejecutar los procesos o por qué tengo que hacer esta tarea... Y te diré lo que pasará si te los saltas... Muahaha! …💀 así que mis historias de terror, como el invierno… están por llegar.

🏛️ Cómocrear un SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) o brindar servicios de SAP AM (Application Management) y realmente vivir felices para siempre con SAP usando Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalización en SAP, ya sea de forma general o por país. ¡Larga vida a SAP Document & Reporting Compliance!

Y también a las extensiones DRC de nuestros partners SAP.

🧭 Roadmaps de soluciones y estrategia de transformación, etc

¿Y cualquier otra cosa? y ... ¡si no sé la respuesta! 👉 Prometo encontrar a alguien que sí la sepa… para ustedes.

 

Ya tenemos todo listo. 💬 Ahora es tu turno.

Deja tu pregunta en la sección de comentario👇 o puedes preguntar de forma anónima aquí 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions o escaneando el QR Code 

 

📅Esta edición del AMA estará abierta para preguntas hasta el 27 de marzo de 2026.

Después de esa fecha, las preguntas seleccionadas serán respondidas en un video grabado que se publicará en el canal de YouTube de la SAP Community  👉 @SAPCommunity

También se publicará un blog resumen con las preguntas y respuestas para la comunidad.

⚠️Solo se considerarán las preguntas enviadas dentro de esta ventana de tiempo.

 

Cuéntame…

🤓¿Qué tema de SAP te gustaría que exploremos juntos?  ESPAÑOL o INGLÉS.

entry.title: Unable to change movement type in Freight Order Booking for Ocean - S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community experts,

I'm experiencing an issue with Freight Order Booking for Ocean shipments in export scenarios. The shipment stages are not updating accordingly.

The scenario operates under multiple Incoterms, including CIF and FOB. However, I’m unable to trigger the correct movement type (e.g., Door-to-Port) based on the Incoterm in order to generate the required stages, specifically pre-carriage and main carriage.

We are receiving the error: Message no. /SCMTMS/OM171. Movement type can't be changed. Requirement documents are assigned.

1. I entered the Incoterm FOB in the sales order, expecting it to trigger the Door-to-Port movement type so that the stages would appear as pre-carriage and main carriage only. However, this did not happen. Additionally, I was not able to select any of the available ports created in the Manage Location app in the Inco. Location 1 field on the Sales tab.

The question is what are the configurations involved and steps needed to achieve each expected movement type from the incoterms selected?

entry.title: リアルユーザ監視のアラート画面について
entry.content:
アラート"Http Error Rate"のアラート詳細画面に表示されている
以下の項目の意味を確認させていただきたいです。

  1. エラー率の算出方法
    エラー率はどのような計算式で算出されているのでしょうか。
  2. 現在のエラー率
    この値は、アラート画面を開いた時点の値でしょうか
  3. 依頼「/sap/bc/srt/rfc/sap･･･」
    アラート詳細に表示される「ステータス」「カウント」などの項目は、何を表しているのでしょうか

詳細は添付ファイルのスクリーンショットをご参照ください。


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: How to Use


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: リアルユーザ監視のアラート画面について
entry.content:
ご担当者様
平素よりお世話になっております。

ご質問につきまして、以下の通り回答申し上げます。

1. エラー率の算出方法

エラー率はどのような計算式で算出されているのでしょうか。

⇒エラー率は、以下の計算式により算出されます。

エラー率 = エラーが発生した HTTP リクエスト数 / HTTP リクエスト総数

2. 現在のエラー率

この値は、アラート画面を開いた時点の値でしょうか。

⇒はい、ご認識の通りでございます。
詳細につきましては、以下のKBAをご参照いただけますと幸いです。

3674648 - What is the timeframe for "error rate" under the alert details of Real User Monitoring? - SAP for Me 
3.依頼「/sap/bc/srt/rfc/sap･･･」
>アラート詳細に表示される「ステータス」「カウント」などの項目は、何を表しているのでしょうか
⇒

アラートページでは、対象範囲に含まれるシステムおよびサービスにおいて、HTTP(S) リクエスト実行時に発生したエラー数が表示されます。

各システムまたはサービスについて、選択された時間範囲において、以下のデータが表示されます。

  • HTTP(S) リクエストの実行回数

  • 呼び出しの成功率（％）

  • HTTP ステータスコード 4xx（クライアントエラー） が発生した呼び出しの割合

  • HTTP ステータスコード 5xx（サーバーエラー） が発生した呼び出しの割合

また、History セクションでは、HTTP(S) 呼び出しおよびエラーの時間推移が表示されます。
概要を分かりやすくするため、選択された時間範囲の前の調整期間もチャートに含まれます。なお、選択された時間範囲自体は紫色でハイライト表示されます。

さらに、テーブル内のシステムまたはサービスをクリックすると、選択したシステムまたはサービスにおけるリクエスト名ごとの詳細データをご確認いただくことが可能です。

以上となります。
ご不明点等がございましたら、お気軽にお知らせください。

何卒よろしくお願い申し上げます。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Leverage System Job Live Model to display Acquired Model Last Refreshed Time in Story
entry.content:
Hi @William_Yu1 ,

thanks for the wonderful article and the detailed explanation about the setup.

While this feature looks very useful, it still doesn’t give us the complete picture. For example, if my model has three import jobs (loading data from flat files), one for each version 1) Actual, 2) Forecast, and 3) Budget and we want to know when each of these versions was last refreshed, we currently don’t get that level of detail. 

Additionally, the Log End Time is only available in the UTC time zone, for global teams working in different regions, it would be much easier to interpret the logs if timestamps could be shown in the user’s local time zone or if a time zone conversion option were available.

Are there any plans for SAP to enhance this capability in the future, for example:
• Providing version-level refresh visibility for import jobs
• Allowing log timestamps to be displayed in the user’s local time zone

These enhancements would significantly improve monitoring and troubleshooting of data loads.

entry.title: ADP GlobalView Acess to Terminated Employees
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m reaching out to understand how other organizations manage access for terminated employees to their payslips, tax documents, and other historical records in ADP GlobalView.

Specifically, we would like to learn:

  1. How do you provide access to former employees after their work accounts are disabled?

    • Do they continue accessing ADP GlobalView through a self-service portal?
    • Do you extend access for a certain period, or do you remove access immediately?

  2. Are former employees allowed to update their login details from their previous work email to a personal email address to retrieve their documents?

    • If yes, how is this handled during offboarding?
    • Are there any controls or verification steps to ensure identity validation?

  3. Do you offer any alternative delivery methods (e.g., email, physical mail, dedicated HR request process) for providing payslips and tax documents to terminated employees?

Any insights, best practices, or process examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you,
Jess

entry.title: How I can Delete the Duplicate Headers in Crystal Reports
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working on a Crystal Reports subreport (RPT file) that displays transportation path data with Primary and Secondary Path sections.

Here is my report structure:
- Report Header b: Column headers (Point of Receipt, Point of Delivery, Path MDQ, Commodity Rate, Fuel Use and Line Loss, Path MDQ Overrun Rate)
- Details a: Primary data row (suppress: {@IsSecondary} = 1)
- Details d: "Primary Path" label
- Details e: Primary column headers
- Details g: Secondary data row (suppress: {@IsSecondary} = 0)
- Details h: "Secondary Path" label
- Details i: Secondary column headers

My @IsSecondary formula:
If UPPERCASE({QPTM_KCTRL_RFS_SO_LOC_VW.CAP_TYPE_CD}) in ["SS","SP"] Then 1 Else 0

The report groups by RFS_NO. Each RFS group can have:
1. Only primary path records (CAP_TYPE_CD not SS or SP)
2. Both primary AND secondary path records

Required output:
- When ONLY primary exists: Show plain table with column headers only, NO "Primary Path" label
- When BOTH exist: Show "Primary Path" label + column headers + data, then "Secondary Path" label + column headers + data

The problem: Report Header b is causing a duplicate column header on the first RFS group. When I suppress Report Header b, some RFS groups lose their column headers entirely.

Current Details e suppress formula:
({@IsSecondary} = 1)
OR
(Previous({QPTM_KCTRL_RFS_SO_LOC_VW.RFS_NO}) = {QPTM_KCTRL_RFS_SO_LOC_VW.RFS_NO}
AND Previous({@IsSecondary}) = 0
AND NOT OnFirstRecord)

How do I suppress Report Header b while ensuring every RFS group still shows the column header row correctly?

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Integration with Qualtrics - Part 1: Setting Up the Employee Global Directory
entry.content:
Organizations today are increasingly focused on understanding and improving the employee experience across the entire lifecycle - from the moment a candidate accepts an offer to the day they exit the organization. SAP SuccessFactors serves as the system of record for people data, while Qualtrics Employee Experience (EX) provides the tools to capture, analyze, and act on employee feedback.

The integration between these two platforms enables organizations to:

  • Automatically trigger surveys based on HR events (new hire, learning completion, promotion, exit, etc.)
  • Personalize surveys with employee attributes (department, location, manager, job role)
  • Eliminate manual processes — no more spreadsheets, CSV uploads, or email lists

But none of this is possible without first establishing the Employee Global Directory in Qualtrics - the central contact repository that powers all survey distribution and reporting.

In this 3-part blog series, we will cover:

 

BlogTopic
Blog 1 (This Blog)Prerequisites & Creating the Employee Global Directory
Blog 2Automating New Hire Surveys
Blog 3Automating Learning Completion Surveys

Let's start with the foundation.

Prerequisites

SuccessFactors

#Prerequisite
1Upgrade Center permissions configured
2Admin RBP permissions assigned
3SF SFTP server access & credentials

Qualtrics

#Prerequisite
1Active Qualtrics license (EX / Lifecycle + XM Directory)
2Administrator / Directory Administrator access
3Workflows feature accessible

Understanding the Integration Approach

Unlike API-based real-time integrations, this approach uses a file-based integration via SFTP (Secure File Transfer Protocol) with PGP encryption for secure data exchange.

SAP provides a pre-built business scenario in Integration Center called: "Qualtrics Integration Definition - EC User Export to SFTP". This template comes with pre-configured source entities, field mappings, and SFTP destination settings - significantly reducing setup effort.

High-Level Flow:

Step-by-Step Configuration

Step 1: Enable Qualtrics from Upgrade Center

This is the first and most critical step. Before you can use the Qualtrics business scenario in Integration Center, you must enable Qualtrics from the Upgrade Center.

1.1 Set Up Permissions for Upgrade Center

Before accessing the Upgrade Center itself, your admin user needs the right permissions.

  1. Make sure this permission is assigned to your user

1.2 Enable Qualtrics in Upgrade Center

  1. Go to Upgrade Center -> Optional Upgrades ->  Qualtrics Employee Experience -> Upgrade now
  2. The Enter Qualtrics Server URL with the format https://<tenant_URL>.qualtrics.com. -> Validate
  3. Check the validation result:
    • Result Message Action
Valid"The URL is valid. Select Submit to continue the upgrade."Click Submit to complete the upgrade
Not Valid"The URL you entered isn't valid. Enter the Qualtrics URL associated with your Qualtrics license and try again, or select Cancel to cancel the upgrade."Verify you've entered the correct URL associated with your Qualtrics license → Click Validate again, or click Cancel to cancel the upgrade

Where to find your Qualtrics URL:

  • Log in to your Qualtrics account
  • Check the browser address bar - the base URL is your tenant URL
  • Example: https://mycompany.qualtrics.com
  • If unsure, contact your Qualtrics Account Manager or check your Qualtrics license agreement

1.3 Verify Enablement

After successful submission, verify:

Check Expected

Upgrade Center → Qualtrics Employee ExperienceStatus: Enabled / Upgraded
Admin Center → Integration Center → Business Scenarios"Qualtrics Integration Definition - EC User Export to SFTP" is now visible

Note: It may take a few minutes for the business scenario to appear in Integration Center after enabling in Upgrade Center.

Step 2: Import PGP Encryption Keys

PGP encryption secures the employee data file during transfer. While PGP encryption is optional in the integration setup, we strongly recommend enabling it as a security best practice for protecting sensitive employee data.

2.1 Retrieve the PGP Public Key that was downloaded from Qualtrics in Step 4.6 during the workflow configuration.

2.2 In SAP SuccessFactors, navigate to Admin Center → Security Center → PGP File Encryption Key.

2.3 Click "Choose File" and select the downloaded PGP Public Key from your local folder.

2.4 Give the name and click "Import key" to upload the key into SuccessFactors.

Step 3: Configure the "Qualtrics Integration Definition - EC User Export to SFTP" integration in SuccessFactors.

3.1 - Search for for business scenario --> Filter with the name "Qualtrics Integration Definition - EC User Export to SFTP". Toggle the "Active" switch to enable the integration, then click "Edit" to configure the settings.

 

 

 

 

 

3.2 - The required fields will be preconfigured as part of the standard business scenario. If any additional fields are required for your organization's reporting or survey personalization needs, they can be added by clicking the "+" button in the field selection screen.

3.3 - Click on "Payload Preview" to generate a preview of the exported data. Save the previewed data in CSV format. This CSV file will be used later in Qualtrics during the workflow creation process, where it will be required to update the file import template and map the incoming fields from SuccessFactors to the corresponding Qualtrics directory contact fields.

3.4 - Apply any necessary filters in the "Filter and Sort" tab. For example, you may want to filter by employment status (active employees only), company code, country, or other organizational attributes to control which employee records are exported.

3.5 - Navigate to the "Destination Settings" tab and maintain the SFTP connection details where the exported files will be placed. This includes:

  • SFTP Host
  • Port
  • File Path / Directory
  • Credentials

Under the "Advanced Settings" section, in the File Encryption configuration, select the PGP public key that was imported in Step 2.2 to ensure the exported employee data file is encrypted before transmission.

3.6 - Navigate to the "Scheduling" tab and configure the job schedule according to your organization's requirements. Common scheduling options include:

  • Daily - recommended for keeping the directory up to date
  • Weekly - suitable for organizations with less frequent employee data changes
  • Define the preferred time of execution (e.g., early morning hours to minimize system load)

3.7 - Navigate to the "Review and Run" tab to verify all configuration settings. Once confirmed, click "Save" to preserve the configuration, then click "Set Schedule" to activate the scheduled job.

Step 4: Creating the Workflow for Employee Global Directory in Qualtrics

4.1 Log in to Qualtrics

Log in to your Qualtrics account with Administrator privileges. From the top navigation menu, click on Workflows. Click Create a Workflow to begin setting up a new automated workflow.

4.2 Configure Workflow Type

Choose "Started at a specific time" as the workflow type. Provide a meaningful name for the workflow (e.g., "SF EC Employee Directory Sync") and select "Stand Alone Workflow" under the "Where to create this workflow" option.

4.3 Set the Workflow Frequency

Select the frequency at which the workflow should run (e.g., Daily or Weekly) based on your organization's requirements. Ensure that the schedule aligns with the Integration Center job schedule configured in Step 3.6, allowing sufficient buffer time for the file to be generated, encrypted, and transferred to the SFTP server. Click "Save" to confirm the schedule.

4.4 Configure the Data Source

Click on the "+" (Add a Task) button and select "Extract data from SFTP files" as the task type. This task will connect to the SFTP server where SuccessFactors places the encrypted employee data file.

4.5 Connect to the SuccessFactors SFTP Server

Configure the SFTP connection by adding the SuccessFactors SFTP account details:

  1. Click "+ Add a user account"
  2. Select "Connect account"
  3. Enter the required SFTP credentials:

4.6 Configure File Import Settings

Provide the following details for the file import configuration:

  1. File Name - Enter the file name or file name pattern of the exported file from SuccessFactors
  2. SFTP Directory - Specify the directory path on the SFTP server where the file is located

Under File Import Settings, configure the following:

  • Select "Decrypt files before importing using PGP" to enable PGP decryption
  • Click "Download the PGP Public Key"

Important: This downloaded PGP Public Key is the key that will be used in Step 2.2 to configure file encryption in SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center.

Next, configure the file settings:

  • File Type: CSV

  • CSV Delimiter: Comma

  • Select the appropriate Post Processing Behavior

Finally, upload the sample CSV file downloaded from the Payload Preview in Step 3.3. This allows Qualtrics to understand the file structure and column headers. Click "Save" to confirm the configuration.

4.7 Configure the Destination

dd a destination task to the workflow to load the extracted employee data into the Qualtrics directory:

  1. Click "Add a Task"
  2. Select "Load Users into EX Directory"
  3. Choose the Data Source from the dropdown (this should reference the SFTP extract task configured in the previous step)
  4. Upload the sample file from Step 3.3 to define the file structure

Next, perform the field mapping by mapping each source field (from the CSV) to the corresponding target field in the Qualtrics directory.

If a required target field does not already exist in the directory, create it by clicking "+ Add New Field" and defining the field name and type.

After completing the field mapping, click "Save".

After completing the field mapping, click Save.

To prevent the workflow from processing empty files, add a validation condition to check that the number of rows to import is not equal to 0. This ensures that the workflow only proceeds when valid employee data is present in the file.

Below is the overall view of the completed workflow:

4.8 Execution logs

  • The execution logs for each workflow run can be accessed under the "Run History" tab within the workflow. This provides details on:

    • Execution status (Success, Failed, Skipped)
    • Number of records processed
    • Errors or warnings encountered during execution
    • Timestamp of each run

    Notifications: Once the job is completed, an email notification will be automatically triggered and sent to the respective stakeholders maintained under the "Sharing & Permissions" section of the workflow. This ensures that the relevant team members are promptly informed of:

    • Successful directory sync completions
    • Any failures or errors requiring attention

 

Conclusion

In this blog, we successfully established the foundation for integrating SAP SuccessFactors with Qualtrics by creating an automated Employee Global Directory sync.

Reference:

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/integrating-sap-successfactors-with-qualtrics/integrating-sap-successfactors-with-qualtrics

What's Next?

In Blog 2, we will leverage this directory to configure an automated New Hire Survey - triggering onboarding feedback surveys directly from SuccessFactors events into Qualtrics.

Stay tuned!

entry.title: Step by step guide to creating your first iFlow in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Introduction

If you are new to SAP Cloud Integration, creating your first iFlow may feel a little overwhelming. But don't worry once you understand the basic building blocks (Sender, Steps, Receiver), and how to deploy and monitor you will be able to build real-world integrations confidently.

In this blog, I will walk you through a beginner-friendly iFlow that exposes a HTTP endpoint and returns a simple response - the fastest way to understand the full lifecycle: design --> deploy --> test --> monitor.

What you will learn

By the end of this blog, you will know how to:

  • Create integration package
  • Create and design iFlow
  • Configure HTTPS Sender
  • Use a content modifier to set a response payload
  • Deploy an iflow
  • Test the endpoint using Postman
  • Monitor the message processing 

Prerequisites

Before starting you should have:

  • Access to SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration capability)
  • Necessary authorizations to create and deploy artifacts (at least integration developer permissions)

Scenario Overview

We will create an iFlow that:

  • Receives a request via HTTPS
  • Sets a response message: "Hello SAP CPI"
  • returns it back to the caller (synchronous)

Step by step guide: Build your first iFlow

Step 1: Go to integration flow designer

  • Open 'SAP Integration Suite'.
  • Navigate to: Design --> Integrations and APIs

  •  

Step 2: Create an Integration Package

  • Click 'Create'.
  • Provide: Name and Short Description

  •  

  • Click "Save".

Step 3: Create an Integration Flow(iFlow)

  • Open your package --> Go to 'Artifacts' tab.
  • Click on 'Add' and select 'Integration flow'

  •  

  • Enter 'Name' for the iFlow and click 'Add'
  •  

     

  • Now open the artifact you named and click on 'Edit' to start designing.

Step 4: Configure the Sender (HTTPS)

  • Click the 'Sender' and connect it to 'Start (Start Message)'.
  • In the Adapter selection choose 'HTTPS'.
  • Configure in 'Connection'  

  •  

  • Provide 'Address': /first and uncheck 'CSRF Protected' to simplify HTTPS sender adapter setup by allowing direct state-changing requests without the extra token fetch step.

Step 5: Add a Content Modifier to set the response

Now we will create the response body

  • From the palette, drag 'Content Modifier' into the flow.
  • Connect it between 'Start' and 'End'.
  • Click the 'Content Modifier' and go to 'Message Body' tab.
  • In 'Body' write the message. 

Step 6: Save and deploy the iFlow

  • Click 'Save'
  • Click 'Deploy'
  • Deployment publishes the iFlow  to runtime, so it becomes executable.

Step 7: Find the Endpoint URL

After deployment:

  • Go to Monitor / Integrations and APIs
  • Open 'Manage Integration Content'.
  • Select your deployed iFlow
  • Copy the endpoint URL shown for the HTTPS sender

Step 8: Testing your iFlow

To test the iFlow, I used Postman, which is a tool for sending HTTP requests to APIs.

  • Open Postman
  • Click 'New Request'
  • Choose the request method.
  • Paste the iFlow Endpoint URL.
  • Add authentication details if required.
  • Click 'Send'.
  • This will trigger the iFlow.

If everything configured correctly, SAP CPI returns a response:

This confirms that the iFlow is deployed successfully and is able to receive and respond to requests.

Note: If you receive errors such as 401, 403, or 404, verify endpoint URL, deployment status and authentication settings.

Step 9: Monitor Message Processing

To confirm CPI processed your message:

  • Go to Monitor / Integrations and APIs
  • Open 'Monitor Message Processing'
  • Find your 'Artifact Name' and check for 'Status'.
  • Monitoring is where you troubleshoot and prove your iFlow is working in runtime.

Optional: Use 'Trace' for deep debugging

If you want to see payload in each step:

  • Go to 'Manage Integration Content'
  • Select your iFlow --> set Log level = Trace
  • Trigger the iFlow again
  • Go back to message monitor and open 'Trace' view.

Note: Trace expires after a short time and trace data is cleared after a retention period, so use it only during debugging.

Conclusion

Creating your first iFlow in SAP CPI is a great starting point for understanding how integrations work in SAP Integration Suite. Even with a simple example like this, you can gain practical experience in designing, deploying, testing and monitoring an integration flow. Once you are comfortable with these basics, you can move on to more advanced topics such as message transformation, routing, and connecting external systems.

entry.title: Incident Management with AI Adapter and ServiceNow in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction:

With the latest capabilities in SAP Integration Suite, organizations can make incident handling much smarter and faster. By combining the AI Adapter with the ServiceNow Adapter, integration flows can automatically analyze errors and create incident tickets without human intervention.

Instead of developers or support teams manually reviewing logs and raising tickets, Generative AI can interpret the error, understand the issue, and automatically trigger the creation of incident in ServiceNow. This makes the entire exception-handling process more efficient and responsive.

By introducing AI into the integration workflow, organizations can significantly reduce manual effort and lower the Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) for critical integration failures.

 

Disclaimer: This blog includes extensive step-by-step explanations and several screenshots, which may make the article longer than usual. 

 

Concept Overview:

The architecture follows three simple steps:

1. Capture the Error Messages

It’s best to create a generic iflow to handle the entire process of automating the ticket creation in ServiceNow from the error generated in other iflows to be directed using JMS or processDirect adapters

2. Analyze and Generate the Prompt

Using the AI Adapter, the error payload is sent to a Large Language Model (LLM). The model analyzes the stack trace or error message and generates:

  • A simplified explanation of the issue
  • Possible root causes
  • Suggested resolution steps

3. Raise Ticket using ServiceNow Adapter

The enriched information is then sent to the ServiceNow Adapter, which automatically creates a structured incident ticket containing the AI-generated analysis.

AI Adapter Variants:

The AI Adapter in SAP Integration Suite currently supports two variants:

  1. AICore-GenAIHub
  2. Custom-OpenAI-REST

AICore-GenAIHub

The AICore-GenAIHub variant integrates with the SAP AI Core service via the SAP Generative AI Hub. SAP GenAI Setup

The Generative AI Hub provides a central gateway to multiple generative AI models from different providers, allowing organizations to consume and manage AI models through SAP’s AI infrastructure on BTP. This enables us to switch between

To use this variant:

  • SAP AI Core service must be configured in the BTP tenant.
  • It supports OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication
  • The service is available only in enterprise or extended environments, not in trial accounts.

Since this setup is not available in the trial landscape, this article will focus on the Custom-OpenAI-REST variant.

Custom-OpenAI-REST

The Custom-OpenAI-REST variant allows integration with AI models that are not hosted or managed by SAP.

This option is used when an AI platform exposes OpenAI-compatible APIs but is not directly supported by SAP’s predefined adapter configurations.

Key characteristics:

  • Configuration Type: Basic or Advanced
  • Connects to OpenAI-compatible endpoints
  • Uses standard REST APIs such as
    • /chat/completions
    • /responses
  • Supports API Key based authentication using Secure Parameters

This enables integration with external AI providers such as OpenAI, Google, or other platforms that expose OpenAI-style APIs.

Prerequisites:

Before building this integration scenario, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  1. Google AI Studio account

    Choose an AI model and generate an API key.

    In this example, we will use the Gemini 1.5 Flash model available via Google AI Studio.

  2. ServiceNow account and Instance

    A ServiceNow account and an active instance are required to create and manage incident tickets through the ServiceNow adapter.

Note: Since I’m using the Gemini AI model, I will configure the adapter using the Advanced configuration type. I will also explain the Basic configuration type and highlight the key differences between the two.

Subscribe to the Gemini Flash Model

First, log in to Google AI Studio and generate an API key that will be used in our IFlow.

After successfully logging in, navigate to the Dashboard section to proceed with the API key creation.

 

Next, go to the Projects section and create a new project by providing an appropriate name. Navigate to the Keys section and create a new key.

Click on the Create API key

Your key has now been generated. Make sure to save it, as it will be required later.

Alternatively, you can click on “Copy cURL quickstart”, which will provide the complete URL, API key, and request body, as shown below.

curl "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-flash-latest:generateContent" \
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-H 'X-goog-api-key: <Your API Key>' \
-X POST \
-d '{
"contents": [
{
"parts": [
{
"text": "Explain how AI works in a few words"
}
]
}
]
}'

ServiceNow Instance Creation:

Now let’s set up the ServiceNow Developer account and create an instance. Visit the ServiceNow Developers page and create an account using your e-mail address. Once you verify your e-mail, you will be redirected to the following screen.

Select the instance of your choice. In my case, I’m selecting the Zurich instance.

Your personal developer instance has now been created, and you will be able to see the generated username and password. We will use these credentials in the ServiceNow Adapter for Basic Authentication

Now that we have the credentials, we need the API details to create an incident in ServiceNow. Log in to your instance using the REST API Explorer | ServiceNow. Once you open it, you will be able to see the list of available resources.

  1. API Name: Select Table API.
  2. Choose the Create a Record (POST) option.
  3. Copy the URL and replace {tablename} with the actual table name (in our case, the incident table).
  4. Scroll down and select the fields that need to be included in the request body.

Note: In ServiceNow, all data is stored in tables. It is also possible to create an empty incident without passing any data in the request body.

As shown below, a ticket is created even when no data is provided in the request body.

To view the list of created incidents, go to Workspaces, search for Incidents, select All Results, and under Service Decks, click on Incidents.

IFlow Process Flow Overview

Finally, let’s take a look at our IFlow and understand how we will use the above setup in our adapters.

Integration Flow

As the Sender, we have used the HTTPS adapter and tested the IFlow using Postman. Based on the requirements, this can be changed - for example, the JMS or ProcessDirect adapters can also be used instead of the HTTPS adapter.

Next comes the key part. In the Content Modifier, I have explicitly defined the prompt that will be sent to the AI model.

AI Model Prompt Content Payload

You can customize the prompt based on your requirements. Now, let’s look at the AI Adapter and the configuration required for the Custom OpenAI REST variant.

In the Connection tab:

  • Address: Use the base URL of the endpoint.
  • API Key Alias: Specify the secure parameter created in the Security Material. This secure parameter stores the API key for the AI model.

AI Adapter - Connection Settings

In the Processing tab configuration, since I’m not using an OpenAI model like ChatGPT—which provides standard endpoints such as /chat/completions or /responses (we’ll look at this later)—I have selected the Advanced type. This option provides more configuration parameters compared to the Basic type.

  1. HTTP Method – Since we are triggering the process to create an incident, select the POST method.

  2. Relative URL – Copy the relative path from the actual endpoint URL and paste it here:

    /v1beta/models/gemini-flash-latest:generateContent

  3. Query – This is mostly used with the GET method. In our case, it is not applicable, so leave it as the default.

Headers

I created header parameters in the Content Modifier named X-goog-api-key to pass the API key and also added Content-Type: application/json.

Content Modifier Header Properties

Processing Tab:

AI Adapter - Advanced Configuration Type Settings

Let’s also discuss the Basic configuration type.

AI Adapter Basic Configuration Type Setting

 

 Parameter NameDescription
Configuration TypeDetermines how the AI adapter is configured. Basic allows configuration through predefined UI fields such as model and prompt, while Advanced provides more flexibility to define the entire request payload manually.
CategorySpecifies the type of AI capability to be used. Available options include Chat Completions, Responses, and Platform. Each category represents a different set of AI APIs used for conversational AI, structured responses, or advanced platform capabilities such as embeddings and image generation.
Entity TypeRepresents the specific AI resource within the selected category. 
Operation

Defines the action to be executed using the selected entity. The available operations depend on the selected Category and Entity Type.

For example, in Chat Completions, the operation is typically Create a model response for a given conversation. Other entities in the platform category may support operations like create, retrieve, update, or delete resources.

Request Payload Source

Determines how the request payload (prompt and parameters) is passed to the AI model.

Options include:

1. UI Configurable – Allows the prompt and model to be defined directly in the adapter configuration.

2. Exchange Body – Allows the request payload to be dynamically provided from the integration flow, typically using a Content Modifier or Groovy script, and passed as the message body.

Model (when UI Configurable is selected)

Specifies the AI model used to generate the response. The list contains the models available in the configured AI service (for example, GPT-based models).

Prompt (when UI Configurable is selected)

Allows the user to directly define the prompt or instruction that will be sent to the AI model. It can include instructions, questions, or contextual information required for the AI interaction.

Difference between /chat/completions and /response endpoints in OpenAI API

Feature/chat/completions/responses

Purpose

Designed specifically for chat-based interactionsA unified endpoint for all types of AI responses
Input FormatUses messages array (system, user, assistant)Uses a more flexible input structure
Future scopeOlder approach (still supported)Recommended modern API by OpenAI
FunctionalitiesPrimarily text/chatSupports text, images, audio, and tool use

Example:

1. /chat/completions

POST /v1/chat/completions
{
  "model":"gpt-4o-mini",
  "messages": [
    {"role":"system", "content":"You are a helpful assistant."},
    {"role":"user", "content":"Explain SAP CPI in simple terms"}
  ]
}

2. /responses

POST /v1/responses
{
  "model":"gpt-4.1-mini",
  "input":"Explain SAP CPI in simple terms"
}

Key Takeaway

  • /chat/completions → Built specifically for chat-style conversations.
  • /responses → A new unified API that works for multiple AI capabilities and is the recommended approach for new integrations.

As the next step, I created a Groovy script to process the response from the AI and extract and format the relevant content required. If you look at my prompt, I asked the model to return the response in JSON format. However, inside the Content tag, the model returned something like this:

{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "content": {
        "parts": [
          {
            "text": "```json\n{\n  \"ErrorDescription\": \"Access denied due to missing or invalid authentication credentials for the requested resource.\",\n  \"PossibleCauses\": \"Invalid or incorrect username/password was provided; The authentication token or API key is missing; The provided token has expired; The user account is locked or disabled; Insufficient permissions for accessing the resource; Server-side authentication configurations are incorrect; Network issues prevented sending valid credentials.\",\n  \"ResolutionSteps\": \"Verify your username and password are correct; Ensure the authentication token or API key is present and valid; Generate a new authentication token if it has expired; Confirm you are using the correct authentication method; Contact your administrator to check account status and permissions; Review server logs for authentication configuration issues; Check network connectivity to the authentication provider; Clear your browser cache and cookies, then retry the action.\",\n  \"SupportGuidance\": \"If the issue persists after following the resolution steps, please contact your IT support team. Provide them with the exact error message, time of occurrence, and any steps you have already attempted. This information will help expedite the investigation.\",\n  \"IncidentMetadata\": {\n    \"state\": \"In Progress\",\n    \"incident_state\": \"Investigating\",\n    \"impact\": \"2\"\n  }\n}\n```"
          }
        ],
        "role": "model"
      },
      "finishReason": "STOP",
      "index": 0
    }
  ],
  "usageMetadata": {
    "promptTokenCount": 257,
    "candidatesTokenCount": 266,
    "totalTokenCount": 1366,
    "promptTokensDetails": [
      {
        "modality": "TEXT",
        "tokenCount": 257
      }
    ],
    "thoughtsTokenCount": 843
  },
  "modelVersion": "gemini-2.5-flash",
  "responseId": "XvevabT6KcDcqtsP_8GP8Q0"
}

Next, I used a Groovy script to extract only the JSON content present inside the text tag. The output after the Groovy script will contain only the required JSON content. Now, we can use the JSON to XML Converter to transform the below payload into an XML structure.

{
  "ErrorDescription": "Access denied due to missing or invalid authentication credentials for the requested resource.",
  "PossibleCauses": "Invalid or incorrect username/password was provided; The authentication token or API key is missing; The provided token has expired; The user account is locked or disabled; Insufficient permissions for accessing the resource; Server-side authentication configurations are incorrect; Network issues prevented sending valid credentials.",
  "ResolutionSteps": "Verify your username and password are correct; Ensure the authentication token or API key is present and valid; Generate a new authentication token if it has expired; Confirm you are using the correct authentication method; Contact your administrator to check account status and permissions; Review server logs for authentication configuration issues; Check network connectivity to the authentication provider; Clear your browser cache and cookies, then retry the action.",
  "SupportGuidance": "If the issue persists after following the resolution steps, please contact your IT support team. Provide them with the exact error message, time of occurrence, and any steps you have already attempted. This information will help expedite the investigation.",
  "IncidentMetadata": {
    "state": "In Progress",
    "incident_state": "Investigating",
    "impact": "2"
  }
}

In the next Content Modifier, I created a few properties with the Source Type set to XPath. These properties are then used to construct the request payload that will be sent to ServiceNow.

Custom Properties created in Content Modifier

Content Modifier Request Payload for ServiceNow Ticket Creation

Content Modifier Body:

{
    "short_description": "${property.short_description}",
    "description": "${property.PossibleCauses}, ${property.ResolutionSteps}, ${property.SupportGuidance}",
    "state": "${property.state}",
    "incident_state": "${property.incident_state}",
    "impact": "${property.impact}"
}

Finally, let’s look at the ServiceNow Receiver Adapter.

In the Connection tab:

  • Address: Copy and paste your ServiceNow instance URL.
  • Authentication: Select Basic Authentication.
  • Credentials: Enter the user credentials that you created in the Security Material.

ServiceNow Connection Tab Settings

In the Processing tab, we are presented with several additional parameters to configure.

ServiceNow Adapter Processing Tab Configurations

API Version - You can choose from different REST endpoint versions. If you are not certain which version to use, it is recommended to leave it as the default value.

Operation - Several operations are available. Since we are creating an incident, select the Create Object operation.

Table Name - Incident because that were all the ticket are stored in ServiceNow

Display Values - The Display Value option determines how reference field values are returned in the response payload. 

In ServiceNow tables, many fields store internal system identifiers (Sys IDs) instead of human-readable values. The Display Value setting controls whether the adapter returns:

  • the raw system value,
  • the human-readable display value, or
  • both the system value and display value.

Access Data Across Domain - This option allows the adapter to retrieve records from multiple domains within a ServiceNow instance.

  • If disabled, the adapter accesses data only from the current domain.

  • If enabled, it can retrieve records from all available domains, such as Global, IT, or Infrastructure.

Response Field - Specifies the fields or properties to be returned in the ServiceNow response. If multiple fields are required, they should be separated using commas.

Render Response For - Available options are Both, Mobile, and Desktop. This setting determines how the response is formatted based on the client type, ensuring compatibility across different platforms.

Suppress System Fields Auto Generation - This option prevents ServiceNow from automatically generating certain system fields when a record is created. Examples include: sys_id, sys_created_on

Display Raw Values - When Display Raw Values is enabled, the API returns the exact value stored in the database. By default, ServiceNow returns data in a formatted or human-readable format.

Exclude Reference Link - In ServiceNow responses, reference fields often include a link to the related resource. When Exclude Reference Link is enabled, the response returns only the value and removes the reference link.

Let’s now look at the actual difference in the response when all these properties are enabled.

Response Without Enabling the Mentioned Fields

Response when the Mentioned Fields are enabled

This is how these options affect the ServiceNow response payload.

Under the Format section, we have Request and Response options. These are quite straightforward, so I’ll skip a detailed explanation here.

Note: The available properties may vary depending on the selected operation. For more details about the ServiceNow Receiver Adapter, refer to the official documentation. ServiceNow Receiver Adapter | SAP Help Portal

Important Note: Make sure your ServiceNow instance is active and running. If the instance is in sleep mode while testing the IFlow, you may receive a response such as “Instance Hibernating Page.”

End to End testing from Postman

In the first Content Modifier, the error tag is captured and stored in a property. In the message body, the expression ${property.errorMsg} is used so that the prompt dynamically includes the specific error content. This ensures that for each error, the AI model receives a different prompt based on the error message.

Response: 

You can see an incident is created with a number "INC0010027". Now, let’s check ServiceNow to see where the incident is created. As expected, a new entry appears in the incident table.

Conclusion:

By integrating the AI Adapter with ServiceNow in SAP Integration Suite, we can significantly improve how incidents are identified and created within enterprise landscapes. Instead of manually analyzing logs and raising tickets, the integration flow can capture errors, send them to an AI model for analysis, and automatically generate a structured incident in ServiceNow.

entry.title: Clean Core Is a Leadership Discipline, Not a Technical Rule
entry.content:
I recently took an SAP course about Clean Core, and it left me thinking about how people interpret the concept in practice. Most explanations focus on ways to keep the system as simple as possible, avoiding changing the original software too much. Personally, what we keep seeing in real SAP transformations that our clients bring to our table is that Clean Core doesn’t necessarily represent an issue because of architecture, but rather because of people. 

The tension around Clean Core becomes apparent the moment a decision maker asks to adjust something “just a little”, explains that their processes work a bit differently, or insists that there’s only one way things have always been done. Of course, there are several ways to solve the request, but my question is: Should the change be implemented at all in the first place? And here is where our concern starts, because this is not a technical decision, but a leadership one.

Clean Core Is Not About Restricting the Business

Clean Core is sometimes interpreted as saying “no” to the business, as if it were about restricting flexibility or forcing standardization just because. In reality, it has more to do with protecting the future of the system. With the migration to cloud ERP and faster upgrade cycles, customization starts to play a different role. Things that used to be manageable in traditional on-premise systems can become way more complicated in a cloud environment. Besides the problems of costs or upgrades, customizations introduce a deeper issue: it becomes less clear who is responsible for what.

This became clear after assessing many of our clients’ landscapes, where heavily customized processes often resulted in unclear ownership. Who owns the process: the business or IT? Who decides when and what needs to change? Over time, these customizations tend to end up in a strange situation where no one really owns, questions or removes them. 

Clean Core introduces a different kind of conversation before all those issues come up. It pushes organizations to ask whether a customization is necessary at all: Does it really create value or is it just the protection of a legacy habit? Once again, we are talking about leadership decisions that have almost nothing to do with architecture. 

What Pressure to Customize Often Reveals

Across multiple client transformations, what the strongest pressure to customize reveals are organizational dynamics. Sometimes, there are governance gaps, where decisions are made without a clear framework or roadmap. Other times, as mentioned earlier, ownership is unclear and different teams defend their own variations of a process. In other cases, deadlines and internal pressure push teams towards quick fixes as temporary solutions instead of redesigning processes. Finally, sometimes customization simply reflects resistance to change. 

These dynamics don’t disappear just because we introduce better extensibility tools. They tend to improve only when leadership alignment improves, and Clean Core works when teams are clear about when it actually makes sense to move away from standard. The key is being intentional. In the projects we’ve worked on, the teams that manage this better are the ones that pause and ask themselves simple questions like if a customization will create problems later on or if it would make upgrades harder. 

Our Experience

Recently, we worked on a S/4 project for a company that had started using drones to collect field data for forecasting. The goal was to move beyond manual reports and gain more real-time visibility from physical assets. Of course, Clean Core was part of the strategy from the beginning, as the goal was to keep S/4 standard and integrate new capabilities in a sustainable way.

Once the workshops started, the situation became more complicated. The forecasting team suggested adding custom fields in inventory and maintenance documents so the drone data could be stored directly in the S/4 tables. It wasn’t an unreasonable idea, we understood that they just wanted the data where everything else already lived.

With that idea on the table, the conversation started moving forward. If the drone data was already in S/4, why not use it directly in the planning logic? There was even the suggestion of modifying the material planning process so the drone data could automatically adjust demand forecasts. What they were asking for was technically possible, and thinking back on, it would’ve probably been faster to just build it directly in the core. This was exactly the moment where all I’ve stated about Clean Core became a reality for us, we had no other choice but to ask ourselves if this logic should really live inside the S/4 core.

In the end, we decided to keep the drone data outside of S/4 and bring the results back through standard interfaces. Yes, it took a bit more coordination, but the core system stayed clean. 

What that project made clear to us is how these situations usually start. At the beginning everyone agrees with the idea of keeping the system standard. But once the workshops begin, small requests start appearing. None of them sound dramatic and, again, they are technically possible and even make sense. But that’s usually how heavy customization slowly creeps back into the system.

Clean Core as Cultural Discipline

In the end, Clean Core says a lot about how an organization makes decisions. In our experience, it depends less on tools and more on leadership, ownership, and how teams make decisions during a transformation. Staying “clean” is not about having a perfect system, but about pausing before customizing and asking whether the change is truly necessary. 

When Clean Core breaks down, it is rarely because the technology couldn’t support the extension. More often it happens when ownership is unclear or short-term pressure overrides long-term thinking. In that sense, Clean Core ends up revealing how disciplined an organization really is when making technology decisions.

entry.title: PIR created in MD61 for 10 months visible in Production but not in Quality system
entry.content:
We are facing an issue related to PIR visibility between Production and Quality systems.

In the Production system, when we create demand using MD61 for around 10 months, the PIR is correctly created and visible for the entire period. Corresponding planned orders are also generated properly.

However, in the Quality system, when the same demand is entered in MD61 for 10 months, the PIR is only partially visible in MD04, and planning seems to occur only for a limited number of months. 

Additional checks performed:

  • In PPDS (/SAPAPO/RRP3) the demand is visible for the full 10 months

  • Product and location combination appears correct

  • Same material and planning parameters used as in Production

  • No errors during PIR creation in MD61

    But still, the full horizon demand is not considered in the Quality system in MD04.

Any guidance on what additional checks we should perform would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigates to transient $uid path instead of server draft path,
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in a CAP + Fiori Elements OData V4 draft application

Problem:

 When I press Create in one specific config app (AggrConfigService / Aggregator), the draft creation flow breaks.

The UI navigates/binds to a transient client-side path like: AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)

CAP then returns: 404 Invalid resource path "AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=...)" What is strange is that the POST itself succeeds first.

Observed request/response

1) Create request: POST Aggregator Body: {"AggregatorID":"Test"}

Response: HTTP/1.1 204 No Content location: Aggregator.drafts('Test') 2) Immediately after that, UI5 sends: GET Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)?$select=AggregatorID,AggregatorName,HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity,IsActive,IsActiveEntity&$expand=DraftAdministrativeData($select=DraftIsCreatedByMe,DraftUUID,InProcessByUser) Response: HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found {"error":{"code":"404","message":"Invalid resource path \"AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)\""}}

So the backend seems to create the draft successfully and returns a draft location, but the frontend still tries to read via the transient $uid path instead of switching to the canonical draft path.

Environment:

SAP CAP Node.js - @sap/cds 8.9.8 - SAPUI5 1.142.0 - Fiori Elements OData V4 - Draft-enabled service - Cloud Foundry deployment on BTP

Questions:

1) Has anyone seen Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigate to a transient `$uid` path like this after a successful POST?

2) Is there any known issue/pattern where FE object-page routing or binding keeps the transient context instead of resolving to the canonical draft location returned by CAP?

Thanks a lot.

Best Regards,

Muthu

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori Elements SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  OData SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: Re: Reload an Object Page with a different record after a...
entry.content:
If you want to control the UI navigation, you have to make sure your custom action is a front-end action (controller extension). If your action also triggers back-end logic (to get the new batch number?) you can do that from inside the controller extension via `editFlow.invokeAction`, see example here: https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/advancedFeatures/guidance/actions

To control the UI navigation in your controller extension you can use `navigateToRoute`, see example here: https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/controllerExtensions/routing

entry.title: BAPI_PO_CREATE1 PO not selectable in MIGO – missing Conditions tab vs ME21N
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am facing an issue with purchase orders created viaBAPI_PO_CREATE1.

When I create a PO manually using ME21N, I can post a Goods Receipt in MIGO without any problem.
However, when I create a PO using BAPI_PO_CREATE1, I get the following error in MIGO:

“Document 450000XXXX does not contain any selectable items.”

What I have checked so far:

  • The PO created via ME21N and the PO created via BAPI have the same header and item data (material, plant, quantity, etc.).
  • The PO created via the BAPI is not delivery completed, not blocked, and fully released.
  • The issue only occurs for BAPI-created POs.

Key difference I noticed:

  • In the PO created via ME21N, the Conditions tab exists and pricing conditions (e.g. PB00 / PBXX) are present.
  • In the PO created via BAPI_PO_CREATE1, the Conditions tab is missing.

What I tried:

  • Populating POCOND / POCONDHEADER and their corresponding X structures (POCONDX, POCONDHEADERX)

However, the Conditions tab is still missing, and the PO items are not selectable in MIGO.

My question:

  1. Is there something specific that must be populated in BAPI_PO_CREATE1 to ensure pricing is finalized (similar to ME21N)?
  2. Are there mandatory fields or flags (e.g. pricing-related fields, CHANGE_ID, NET_PRICE, PRICE_UNIT, etc.) that are commonly missed when creating POs via the BAPI?

Any guidance or references to relevant SAP Notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Calculation formula of Run Accrual/Deferral for CP
entry.content:
Could you tell us the calculation formula of Run Accrual/Deferral for CP?



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Calculation formula of Run Accrual/Deferral for CP
entry.content:
Pro Rata with Linear Discounting
This method is only used for Commercial Paper. The total amount is first accrued/deferred (pro rata) over the calculation period and then discounted on a linear basis. The system uses the following formula to calculate the discounting factor:

For accrual:
1 + interest rate of profit-based flow / 100 * (days in period- D) / base days

For deferral:
1 + interest rate of profit-based flow / 100 * D / base days

entry.title: PO created via BAPI_PO_CREATE1 cannot be used for GR in MIGO, but ME21N PO works

Hi everyone,

I am having an issue with purchase orders created using BAPI_PO_CREATE1.

If I create a PO manually with ME21N, I can post a goods receipt in MIGO without any issues. However, if I create a PO using BAPI_PO_CREATE1, I get the following error in MIGO when trying to post GR:

“Document 450000XXXX does not contain any selectable items.”

I compared a PO created via ME21N and one created via the BAPI. Header and item data such as material, plant, quantity, etc. are the same, and the BAPI-created PO is not blocked, not delivery completed, and fully released.

One clear difference I noticed is that the PO created via ME21N has a Conditions tab with pricing conditions maintained, while the PO created via BAPI_PO_CREATE1 does not have the Conditions tab at all.

I tried populating POCOND and POCONDHEADER (as well as the corresponding X structures) when calling the BAPI, but this did not change the result. The Conditions tab is still missing, and the PO items are still not selectable in MIGO.

My questions are:

  • Can missing or incomplete pricing/conditions cause PO items to be non‑selectable for goods receipt in MIGO?
  • Is there anything specific that needs to be populated in BAPI_PO_CREATE1 to ensure pricing is finalized in the same way as ME21N?
  • Are there any commonly missed mandatory fields or flags related to pricing (for example NET_PRICE, PRICE_UNIT, CHANGE_ID, etc.) when creating POs via the BAPI?

Any advice or pointers would be appreciated. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Designing an External Career Site in SAP SuccessFactors SmartRecruiters
entry.content:
In today’s competitive hiring landscape, a well-designed career site plays a crucial role in attracting top talent. A career site is often the first interaction candidates have with an organization, making it essential to create an engaging, informative, and user-friendly experience.

SmartRecruiters provides powerful tools to design and manage an External Career Site that reflects the organization’s employer brand while offering seamless job search and application experiences.

This blog explains the design approach, configuration steps, and page-linking process required to build a functional, visually appealing SmartRecruiters external career site.

Overview of SmartRecruiters External Career Site

The External Career Site in SmartRecruiters is the public-facing portal where candidates can:
• Search and apply for open jobs
• Learn about the company culture
• Explore teams and locations
• Understand benefits and values
• Join talent communities

The platform allows organizations to create a fully branded career site with customizable pages, navigation menus, and content blocks.

Key capabilities include:
• Employer branding customization
• Responsive design for mobile devices
• Dynamic job search functionality
• Custom landing pages
• Page linking and navigation control
• SEO optimization for job visibility

Key Components of a SmartRecruiters Career Site

Before configuring the site, it is important to understand the main structural components.

1. Homepage

The homepage acts as the main entry point and typically includes:
• Hero banner or video
• Job search widgets
• Company highlights
• Links to key page
• Job Listing

2. Content Pages

These pages provide employer branding content such as:
• About Us
• Jobs for Students & Graduates
• Jobs for Experienced Professionals
• Job Our Talent Community

Configuration Steps to Design an External Career Site

Below is a step-by-step approach to configure and design the site.

Step 1: Access Career Site Builder
1. Log in to SmartRecruiters Admin
2. Navigate to: Settings → Career Pages & Job ad under Career Page Setup

3. Click on the settings icon for the default career site to configure or create a new one.

Here you can manage:
• Site design
• Pages
• Navigation
• Branding elements

Step 2: Configure Branding and Theme

The first step in designing a career site is aligning it with the company’s employer brand.
Configuration includes:

• Upload company logo – Click on logo section, upload new logo, and click on Publish button.

 

• Homepage hero image or video – Click on the Homepage Hero Image section, upload the image, and click on the Publish button.

• Define brand colors – You can click on the edit screen anywhere and update the Brand/Corporate colors.

• Configure Headers – Click on the Header section, add the top navigation links image, and click on the Publish button.

• Add new section – Click on add new section, select the Article align, update the image, and the content.

Note: Currently, only two alignments are supported, i.e., Left and Right.

Step 3: Create Top Navigation Pages

1. To Create New Pages for the top navigation menu link, the admin can navigate to: Settings → Career Pages & Job ad under Career Page Setup

2. Click on the “Add New” button, update the Name, Language, and filter job to be displayed on the page, and save the page.

Once you save these page details, a Page will appear under Career pages. Admin can click on the “settings” icon to customize the page just like the default or landing page.

Step 4: Add Top Navigation Page Link

Once the Top Navigation Pages are created, the admin can go back to the default page and place the link to the newly created pages.

Click on the Header section in the default Page, add a top navigation label and link, and click on the Publish button.

Admin can also preview the “Career Site” in a different view to check the preview before publishing the site.

Currently, Mobile, Tablet, and Desktop previews are supported in the design screen.

Step 4: Add Talent Community, Signup Link

Admin can also add a Talent Community Signup Link in one of the sections or pages of the career site.

1. Create Talent Community – Admin can go to SmartRecruiters login and click on “Communities” and click on the “Create Community” button to set up a community

2. Enter all the required information for the community and click on “Done”.

3. Create Signup Form – Go to the “Forms” tab of the Talent Community and click on the “Create form” button.

4. Enter all required information in the form, such as Name, Brand, Logo, Description, resume, fields to be filled by the candidate, etc., and click on the “Publish form” button.

Admin can also click on the “Preview” button to see the preview of the form.

5. Once the form is created and published, Admin can go to the “Form” section and copy the URL of the form.

6. Add Talent Community Form link in career site – Admin can open the career site page, default or the top navigation page, add a new section, and select a text to update the hyperlink.

Here is the final output of my demo Career Site:

Conclusion

Designing an External Career Site in SmartRecruiters allows organizations to create a powerful digital gateway for attracting talent. By configuring branding, creating structured pages, and linking content effectively, companies can deliver a seamless candidate journey from job discovery to application submission.

With the right design strategy and configuration approach, SmartRecruiters career sites can significantly enhance both employer branding and recruitment outcomes.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - Automation does not co...
entry.content:
In case anyone else runs into the same issue. 
Go to Monitoring --> Automation Jobs. Remove all filters (especially the Status filter) and click Go. 

You should see your automations that failed there. Make sure that the environment that you deployed the workflow to has an agent registered - you might have your agent registered to the public environment while deploying your workflow to an environment that doesn't have an agent registered.
